message:
test(conformance): differential harness for string.h mutating ops vs glibc

CONFORMANCE: libc string.h mutating diff matrix.

15 functions, 16 tests:
- strlen — 6 cases incl. high bytes
- strcmp — 13 pairs (sign-of-result compare)
- strncmp — 13 pairs × 4 bounds = 52 calls
- strcpy — 4 sources, post-call buffer compare with sentinel pattern
- strncpy — 4 sources × 6 bounds = 24 cases (POSIX zero-fill semantics
  exercised at every n)
- strcat / strncat — 5 + 3 pair sets, dst seeded with prefix
- strdup / strndup — heap allocation, byte equality, then libc::free
  on both pointers
- memcpy — 6 sizes (0/1/7/8/31/256), distinct content per offset
- memmove — overlapping regions, both forward and backward (overlap=±3)
- memset — 5 sizes × 5 c values incl. 0x100 truncation and -1
- strerror — 7 errno codes (presence/non-empty match; messages can
  vary by libc version, documented as XFAIL-relaxed in comment)
- strerror_r (XSI variant via __xpg_strerror_r) — return-value match
- strtok_r — 6 (src, delim) cases incl. adjacent/trailing delims,
  multi-char delim sets, empty source (token sequence equality)

0 divergences observed. string.h mutating + comparison family is
conformant against glibc.

Companion to commit 631b5100 (pure subset). Combined string.h
coverage is now 24 functions (9 pure + 15 mutating).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
